source: Flocabulary G2 + Dolch Sight Words + State Standards

words.definition: a group of people related to each other
words.example: My family eats dinner together every night.

words.definition: a male parent
words.example: My father reads me a story at bedtime.

words.definition: a female parent
words.example: My mother helps me with my homework.

words.definition: a boy or man who has the same parents as you
words.example: My brother is two years older than me.

words.definition: a girl or woman who has the same parents as you
words.example: My sister likes to paint pictures.

words.definition: a building where people live
words.example: Their house has a red front door.

words.definition: a space in a building with walls around it
words.example: I keep my toys in my room.

words.definition: a movable panel used to open or close an entrance
words.example: He knocked on the door before entering.

words.definition: an opening in a wall that lets in light and air
words.example: She looked out the window at the rain.

words.definition: with each other; as a group
words.example: We played together at the park.

words.definition: a person who helps you learn at school
words.example: Our teacher showed us how to add numbers.

words.definition: to get new knowledge or a skill
words.example: We learn new words every day at school.

words.definition: to look at words and understand them
words.example: I like to read books about animals.

words.definition: to put words on paper or a screen
words.example: Please write your name at the top of the page.

words.definition: to make a picture using a pencil or pen
words.example: I love to draw pictures of horses.

words.definition: to use your mind to have ideas
words.example: Think about the answer before you speak.

words.definition: to pay attention to sounds or what someone says
words.example: Please listen to the directions carefully.

words.definition: what you say or write back when asked a question
words.example: She raised her hand to give the answer.

words.definition: something you ask to find out information
words.example: He asked a question about the homework.

words.definition: something that shows what other things are like
words.example: A cat is an example of a pet.

words.definition: a living creature that can move and breathe
words.example: A dog is my favorite animal.

words.definition: a creature with wings and feathers that can usually fly
words.example: A pretty bird sang in the tree.

words.definition: a creature that lives in water and has fins
words.example: We saw many colorful fish at the aquarium.

words.definition: the part at the back of an animal's body
words.example: The happy dog wagged its tail.

words.definition: the part of a bird or insect used for flying
words.example: The butterfly spread its wings and flew away.

words.definition: the soft thick hair on an animal's body
words.example: The rabbit has soft white fur.

words.definition: living in nature, not kept as a pet
words.example: Wild animals live in the forest.

words.definition: an animal that lives with people as a friend
words.example: My pet is a small brown dog.

words.definition: to give food to a person or animal
words.example: We feed the fish every morning.

words.definition: to look after someone or something
words.example: I care for my pet hamster every day.

words.definition: anything that people or animals eat
words.example: Fruit is a healthy food for kids.

words.definition: to swallow a liquid
words.example: Remember to drink water every day.

words.definition: the first meal of the day in the morning
words.example: I had eggs and toast for breakfast.

words.definition: the meal eaten in the middle of the day
words.example: We eat lunch at school at noon.

words.definition: the main meal of the day, usually in the evening
words.example: Our family has dinner at six o'clock.

words.definition: to sense the flavor of food or drink in your mouth
words.example: This apple tastes sweet and juicy.

words.definition: to make food ready to eat by using heat
words.example: My dad loves to cook pancakes on Saturday.

words.definition: to give part of something to someone else
words.example: I like to share my snacks with my friends.

words.definition: feeling that you need to eat
words.example: After playing outside, I was very hungry.

words.definition: having no space left; having eaten enough
words.example: I am full after eating that big lunch.

words.definition: what it is like outside — sunny, rainy, windy, etc.
words.example: The weather today is sunny and warm.

words.definition: bright with light from the sun
words.example: It is a sunny day, so let's play outside.

words.definition: drops of water that fall from clouds in the sky
words.example: The rain made puddles on the sidewalk.

words.definition: soft white frozen water that falls from the sky
words.example: We made a snowman after the snow fell.

words.definition: air that moves around outside
words.example: The wind blew the leaves off the trees.

words.definition: a white or gray shape in the sky made of tiny water drops
words.example: A big cloud covered the sun.

words.definition: a little bit hot; comfortable temperature
words.example: Spring is warm and flowers begin to bloom.

words.definition: having a low temperature; not warm
words.example: Winter is cold and we wear thick coats.

words.definition: one of the four parts of the year: spring, summer, fall, winter
words.example: My favorite season is summer because I can swim.

words.definition: to become different or make something different
words.example: The leaves change color in the fall.

words.definition: to move your legs quickly to go fast
words.example: We run around the playground at recess.

words.definition: to push your body up into the air with your legs
words.example: The frog can jump very high.

words.definition: to go up something using your hands and feet
words.example: We like to climb the big tree in the yard.

words.definition: to hold and move something from one place to another
words.example: Can you carry this bag for me?

words.definition: to move something toward you
words.example: Pull the door to open it.

words.definition: to press something away from you
words.example: Push the button to start the game.

words.definition: to send something through the air with your hand
words.example: Throw the ball to your teammate.

words.definition: to grab something that is moving through the air
words.example: She tried to catch the ball with both hands.

words.definition: to go behind or come after someone
words.example: Follow me and I will show you the way.

words.definition: to carry something to a place
words.example: Please bring your book to class.

words.definition: feeling good and pleased
words.example: She was happy when she got a new puppy.

words.definition: feeling unhappy
words.example: He was sad when his friend moved away.

words.definition: feeling very upset about something
words.example: She felt angry when someone took her crayon.

words.definition: feeling scared
words.example: The little boy was afraid of the dark.

words.definition: feeling very happy and full of energy about something
words.example: We are excited about the field trip tomorrow.

words.definition: feeling good about something you or someone else did well
words.example: Mom was proud of me for getting a good grade.

words.definition: feeling amazed because something unexpected happened
words.example: She was surprised by the birthday party.

words.definition: friendly and caring toward others
words.example: It is kind to share with your friends.

words.definition: making very little or no noise
words.example: Please be quiet during the test.

words.definition: to make sounds that show you think something is funny
words.example: The funny movie made everyone laugh.

words.definition: a place where children go to learn
words.example: I walk to school with my friend every day.

words.definition: an open area with grass and trees for people to enjoy
words.example: We play on the swings at the park.

words.definition: a place where you can buy things
words.example: We went to the store to buy groceries.

words.definition: a place where you can borrow and read books
words.example: I got three new books from the library.

words.definition: a road in a town or city with buildings on each side
words.example: Look both ways before crossing the street.

words.definition: a person who lives near you
words.example: Our neighbor has a friendly dog.

words.definition: to go to see a person or place
words.example: We visit our grandparents every weekend.

words.definition: not in danger; protected from harm
words.example: Always wear a seatbelt to stay safe.

words.definition: a place where many people live and work, smaller than a city
words.example: Our town has a park and a library.

words.definition: to be a member or part of a group
words.example: These books belong to the classroom library.

words.definition: the early part of the day before noon
words.example: I eat breakfast every morning.

words.definition: the time when it is dark outside
words.example: The stars come out at night.

words.definition: on this day
words.example: Today is a beautiful day.

words.definition: the day after today
words.example: We have a test tomorrow.

words.definition: before the usual time
words.example: She woke up early to watch the sunrise.

words.definition: at all times; every time
words.example: She always brushes her teeth before bed.

words.definition: once in a while; not all the time
words.example: Sometimes we go to the beach on weekends.

words.definition: not at any time; not ever
words.example: I have never seen a real whale.

words.definition: to start doing something
words.example: Let's begin the lesson now.

words.definition: to complete something; to come to the end
words.example: I need to finish my homework before dinner.

words.definition: a tall plant with a trunk, branches, and leaves
words.example: We sat under the big tree for shade.

words.definition: the colorful part of a plant that makes seeds
words.example: She picked a yellow flower from the garden.

words.definition: a piece of land where plants and flowers are grown
words.example: We planted tomatoes in our garden.

words.definition: a large stream of water that flows across the land
words.example: The river runs through the middle of town.

words.definition: a very high area of land with steep sides
words.example: We could see snow on top of the mountain.

words.definition: a large area covered with trees
words.example: Many animals live in the forest.

words.definition: the planet we live on; also the soil on the ground
words.example: We should take care of the earth.

words.definition: to get bigger over time
words.example: Plants grow when you water them.

words.definition: a small part of a plant that can grow into a new plant
words.example: We planted a seed and it became a sunflower.

words.definition: not inside a building; in the open air
words.example: Let's go outside and play.

words.definition: a quality like red, blue, or green that you can see
words.example: My favorite color is blue.

words.definition: the outline or form of something
words.example: A ball has a round shape.

words.definition: shaped like a circle or ball
words.example: The pizza is round.

words.definition: little in size; not big
words.example: The mouse is a very small animal.

words.definition: big in size
words.example: An elephant is a very large animal.

words.definition: having a greater height than usual
words.example: The giraffe is the tallest animal.

words.definition: not tall; also not long in time or length
words.example: The little puppy has short legs.

words.definition: giving out a lot of light; strong in color
words.example: The sun is very bright today.

words.definition: having little or no light
words.example: It gets dark early in winter.

words.definition: not the same as another
words.example: Every snowflake is different.

words.definition: to make it easier for someone to do something
words.example: Can you help me find my pencil?

words.definition: a polite word used when asking for something
words.example: Please pass me the salt.

words.definition: to tell someone you are grateful for what they did
words.example: I want to thank you for the gift.

words.definition: feeling bad about something you did
words.example: I am sorry for stepping on your foot.

words.definition: your chance to do something in order
words.example: Wait for your turn to speak.

words.definition: showing good manners; being respectful
words.example: It is polite to say please and thank you.

words.definition: soft and careful; not rough
words.example: Be gentle with the baby kitten.

words.definition: to say that you will definitely do something
words.example: I promise to clean my room after school.

words.definition: what is real and correct; not a lie
words.example: Always tell the truth even when it is hard.

words.definition: to stop being angry at someone for what they did
words.example: I forgive you for breaking my crayon.

words.definition: an exciting or unusual experience
words.example: Going camping in the woods was a great adventure.

words.definition: to do something fun; to take part in a game
words.example: Let's play a board game after dinner.

words.definition: an activity with rules that you do for fun
words.example: We played a fun card game at the party.

words.definition: a set of events told in words; a tale
words.example: Grandma told us a story about a magic fish.

words.definition: sounds arranged in a way that is nice to listen to
words.example: We listened to music and danced.

words.definition: to make music with your voice
words.example: The children sing songs every morning.

words.definition: to move your body to music
words.example: We love to dance at birthday parties.

words.definition: pictures and stories in your mind when you sleep; a hope
words.example: I had a dream that I could fly.

words.definition: to want something very much; to hope for something
words.example: I wish I could have a pet rabbit.

words.definition: better or more important than usual
words.example: Today is a special day because it is my birthday.
